note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. refactor-audit is the WHOLE-PROJECT structural audit — the scope discriminator against refactor-smells (single path/module/diff) is the sharpest boundary; migration-plan (executing a named migration), explain (understanding, not cataloguing debt), and sec-scan (security, not structure) are the other near-misses.

queries.text: run a refactoring audit on the whole repo — where's the tech debt?
we're planning a cleanup sprint next week, what should we refactor first across the codebase?
do a code health check on the project and rank the worst offenders by churn and size
find the hotspots in this repo — the files that change constantly and are huge
my manager wants a structural audit before we commit to paying down tech debt this quarter
map out the architecture and find the dependency tangles and dead code candidates
marvin refactor audit please, whole project
give me a numbered findings register of structural problems across the codebase with severity and effort
where should we start refactoring? I need an evidence-based list, not vibes
audit the codebase structure and tell me which modules are the biggest liabilities
scan src/api/handlers.ts for code smells and anti-patterns
queries.note: single-file scoped scan → refactor-smells, not the whole-project audit

queries.text: any smells in this diff before I push?
we picked findings F2 and F5 from the audit — sequence them into a refactoring plan
queries.note: findings already exist; turning them into steps → refactor-plan

queries.text: apply step 3 of the refactoring plan
plan our migration from the legacy ORM to Prisma with rollback steps
queries.note: named migration with dependency/rollback strategy → migration-plan

queries.text: explain how the payment reconciliation module is architected and why
queries.note: understanding one module's design → explain, not a debt catalogue

queries.text: do a full security audit of the codebase for OWASP Top 10 issues
actually go clean up the duplication in the auth package for me
queries.note: asks to change code, not produce a read-only report → refactor-apply (via plan)

queries.text: check our dependencies for known CVEs and unmaintained packages
queries.note: dependency vulnerability audit → sec-deps, not structural hotspots

queries.text: run the quality gates — tests, lint, type-check — and tell me if the repo is green
queries.note: quality-gate run → task-verify, not a structural audit

queries.text: scan the whole project for code smells and hotspots so we know where the debt is
queries.note: shares 'code smells' vocab with refactor-smells, but scope is whole-project + hotspots → refactor-audit wins

queries.text: before we plan any refactoring, give me the findings — audit the whole codebase's structure first
queries.note: mentions 'plan refactoring' but the requested action is producing findings first → must beat refactor-plan

queries.text: check just the src/billing module for anti-patterns and naming issues
queries.note: scoped to one module → refactor-smells is more specific and must win

queries.text: we're refactoring the storage layer to a new schema — plan the steps, risks, and rollback
queries.note: sequencing refactor steps/risk → refactor-plan (an auto-invocable sibling)
